signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Hawkish Fed Bets
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5638 — USD outperformance (avg +0.811%) vs NZD underperformance (avg -1.691%) creates a 250bp G10 spread. Hawkish Fed bets and Hormuz geopolitical risk drive USD demand despite dialled-back aggressive pricing earlier in the week. NZD real rate at -0.83% (accommodative) vs USD +1.56% (restrictive) reinforces the structural downside. Retail 93% long vs COT LF net short -23,314 confirms a retail-fading-institutional-trend divergence; retail capitulation risk is high if 0.5600 breaks.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Vol + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.72 — 275bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid, but 160.00 remains the critical level for BoJ intervention risk. COT LF net short -115,033 (crowded carry) vs retail 84% short creates a rare alignment where both specs and retail are fading the upside, yet price remains bid. IV 7.2% vs HV30 2.1% (VRP +5.0%) signals the options market is paying a heavy premium for protection against a sharp carry unwind. Carry dominates while VIX remains at 18.4 (normal volatility).

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + CESI
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6893 — COT LF net long +29,035 (64.9% long) faces extreme options hedging as VRP hits +14.6% (IV 22.1% vs HV30 7.5%). While RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% provides a 60bp carry advantage for AUD, the USD CESI (+291) significantly outperforms AUD (+6), suggesting US data resilience is overmatching the carry draw. 25d RR at -0.98 confirms a moderate put skew as the market hedges for a break below 0.6850.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Retail Extreme
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4197 — Correlation with WTI Oil has inverted to -0.732 (z = -2.13σ) vs the 252d norm of +0.024, indicating CAD is decoupling from energy prices to follow the 150bp USD carry advantage. Retail 92% short vs COT LF net short -87,096 shows retail is aggressively fading the move while institutions remain short CAD. Watch for a squeeze toward 1.4300 if US NFP data next week exceeds expectations.
